parsed_review.summary: CP00-736 (RP24 Korean Legal Depth P05 workflow/permission slice, Risk B, 40 units, RP24.P05.M03.S07-RP24.P05.M05.S02) is a clean descriptor-only pack and the production_ready claim is appropriately gated. The CP736 binding (registry.js:247) and requirements (registry.js:2440) exactly match the prompt baseline distribution (claude_review:2, implementation:16, security_audit:4, fixture:10, test:6, hermes_evidence:2; micro-titles 16/22/2). service.js/validators.js build and enforce descriptor-only rows carrying the no-write attestation, runtime-closed flags, and an authority boundary that keeps Claude non-final, requires human approval for runtime opening, and refuses any enterprise-trust claim from local validation. model.test.js:37-39 add CP736-specific coverage, runtime-closed-packet, and contract-latest-projection tests; the regenerated contract reports validation.valid=true with CP00-736 as latest and CP00-722..735 historical. command-evidence.json records the full required validation ladder (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) at exit 0. Boundaries hold: no real client/matter data or secrets in source, writes_product_state=false, no HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate/clause runtime opened, and Hermes is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/review packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false). The manifest/inspection/adjudication correctly show production_ready=false/runtime_ready=false pending exactly this hardened read-only review receipt; the pack claims only descriptor verification, not runtime or enterprise trust. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. This verdict is based on read-only inspection of the recorded artifacts (Read/Grep/Glob); the validation commands were not re-executed in this review.
